blocks.content: Friday afternoon, last class. Ms. Barrett handed back the writing from Tuesday. She'd put a comment at the bottom of mine in red pen.
'The gap between who you want to be and who you are in the moment — that's the whole story. Keep pulling on this thread.'
I folded it and put it in my pocket. That was going in the notebook. When I started one.
After school, main entrance this time. No side exit. Just the front.
Daniel was at the corner with two different guys — not the ones from Thursday. Different faces. He looked up when I came through the doors. Our eyes met for about half a second.
Not retreat. Not done. But something in the math had changed. I kept walking. Didn't slow down, didn't look back.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: That's a different kind of quiet, bro.
I knew what he meant. Daniel was the kind of quiet that meant he was done being obvious. He'd go underground now. Recalculate from somewhere you can't see.

blocks.prompt: Daniel looked away first. Jonathan says it's a different kind of quiet.

options.label: "He went underground. That's his move when something doesn't work."
Keep walking. Don't give it more energy than it deserves.
